---
title: "Assignment 2 - Data Analysis using R Programming"
subtitle: "Historical Stock Analysis of Axis Bank for Revenue Optimization"
author: "Group 5"
date: "2026-04-15"
output: pdf_document
---

# Introduction

This analysis uses 15-minute historical stock data (2025 only) for Axis Bank. Insights on price movements and high-volume trading help the bank optimize trading strategies and generate revenue through better information systems. 1. Load the Dataset

# 1. Load the Dataset

# Click on the NEW file AXISBANK_15m_comma.csv when the pop-up appears
data <- read.csv(file.choose(), header = TRUE)

# Convert number columns
data$open   <- as.numeric(data$open)
data$high   <- as.numeric(data$high)
data$low    <- as.numeric(data$low)
data$close  <- as.numeric(data$close)
data$volume <- as.numeric(data$volume)

# Create Date column
data$Date <- as.Date(substr(data$timestamp, 1, 10))

print("SUCCESS! Column names:")
print(names(data))
print("Number of rows:")
print(nrow(data))

# 2. Print the Structure of the Dataset

str(data)

# 3. List the Variables in the Dataset

names(data)

# 4. Print the Top 15 Rows of the Dataset

head(data, 15)

# 5. User-Defined Function

calc_15m_return <- function(open_price, close_price) {
  return(((close_price - open_price) / open_price) * 100)
}
calc_15m_return(data$open[1], data$close[1])

# 6. Filter Rows (2025 high-volume data)

filtered_data <- data[format(data$Date, "%Y") == "2025" & 
                      data$volume > mean(data$volume, na.rm = TRUE), ]
head(filtered_data, 5)

# 7. Dependent & Independent Variables + New Data Frame

# Dependent: close price (what we want to predict for revenue)
# Independent: volume and open price (predictors)

dep_df <- filtered_data[, c("Date", "close")]
ind_df <- filtered_data[, c("Date", "volume", "open")]

# Simple reshaping (wide to long) - we add a unique ID to avoid the error
filtered_data$id <- 1:nrow(filtered_data)          # temporary unique ID
temp_wide <- filtered_data[, c("id", "Date", "close", "volume")]

long_df <- reshape(temp_wide, 
                   direction = "long",
                   varying = list(c("close", "volume")),
                   v.names = "Value",
                   timevar = "Metric",
                   times = c("close", "volume"),
                   idvar = "id")

# Create new data frame by joining dependent + independent variables (this is the main requirement)
new_df <- merge(dep_df, ind_df, by = "Date")
head(new_df, 5)

# 8. Remove Missing Values

new_df <- na.omit(new_df)

# 9. Remove Duplicated Data

new_df <- new_df[!duplicated(new_df), ] 
nrow(new_df)

# 10. Reorder Rows in Descending Order

ordered_df <- new_df[order(-new_df$close), ]
head(ordered_df, 5)

# 11. Rename Columns

colnames(new_df)[colnames(new_df) == "close"] <- "Closing_Price"
colnames(new_df)[colnames(new_df) == "volume"] <- "Trading_Volume"
head(new_df, 3)

# 12. Add New Variables

new_df$Trading_Volume_Doubled <- new_df$Trading_Volume * 2
new_df$Total_Trade_Value <- new_df$Trading_Volume * new_df$Closing_Price
head(new_df, 3)

# 13. Create Training Set

set.seed(123)
train_index <- sample(1:nrow(new_df), 0.7 * nrow(new_df))
train_set <- new_df[train_index, ]
nrow(train_set)

# 14. Summary Statistics

summary(new_df)

## 15. Mean, Median, Mode, Range

mean_price   <- mean(new_df$Closing_Price, na.rm = TRUE)
median_price <- median(new_df$Closing_Price, na.rm = TRUE)

get_mode <- function(v) {
  uniqv <- unique(v)
  uniqv[which.max(tabulate(match(v, uniqv)))]
}
mode_price <- get_mode(new_df$Closing_Price)

range_price <- range(new_df$Closing_Price, na.rm = TRUE)

# Create a clean table (this works perfectly in PDF)
stats_table <- data.frame(
  Statistic = c("Mean", "Median", "Mode", "Range (Min to Max)"),
  Value     = c(round(mean_price, 2),
                round(median_price, 2),
                round(mode_price, 2),
                paste(round(range_price[1], 2), "to", round(range_price[2], 2)))
)

knitr::kable(stats_table, caption = "Summary Statistics of Closing Price")

# 16. Scatter Plot

plot(new_df$Trading_Volume, new_df$Closing_Price,
     main = "Scatter Plot: Trading Volume vs Closing Price",
     xlab = "Trading Volume", ylab = "Closing Price", pch = 19, col = "blue")

# 17. Bar Plot (Average Closing Price by Weekday)

filtered_data$Weekday <- weekdays(filtered_data$Date)
avg_close <- tapply(filtered_data$close, filtered_data$Weekday, mean, na.rm = TRUE)
barplot(avg_close, main = "Bar Plot: Avg Closing Price by Weekday",
        xlab = "Weekday", ylab = "Average Closing Price", col = "steelblue")

# 18. Pearson Correlation

correlation <- cor(new_df$Trading_Volume, new_df$Closing_Price, method = "pearson")
correlation

# Conclusion

The analysis identifies revenue opportunities for Axis Bank through high-volume trading patterns.
